clinical_question: Evaluation and management of acute ischemic stroke secondary to right M1 MCA occlusion, post-thrombolysis and mechanical thrombectomy, including assessment for complications and long-term stroke prevention strategies.
relevant_history: 62-year-old female with history of atrial fibrillation (not anticoagulated), hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and hyperlipidemia presenting with acute onset left-sided weakness and slurred speech.
pertinent_findings: Sudden onset left hemiparesis (arm 1/5, leg 2/5) and slurred speech starting at 1:30 PM.
GCS 13 (E4V4M5) with right gaze preference and left facial droop.
CT head without contrast: No hemorrhage, no early ischemic changes.
CT angiography: Complete occlusion of the right middle cerebral artery (M1 segment).
current_management: Received IV Tenecteplase 500 mg IV push and underwent emergent mechanical thrombectomy. Currently on IV Labetalol/Nicardipine for BP control, Aspirin 325 mg PO, and insulin drip for glucose management. Admitted to Stroke Unit/Neuro ICU.
specific_asks: Neurological assessment and monitoring for complications (e.g., hemorrhagic transformation, cerebral edema, seizures).
Guidance on blood pressure management post-thrombectomy.
Recommendations for secondary stroke prevention, particularly regarding anticoagulation given the history of atrial fibrillation and recent stroke.
Assessment of eligibility for and timing of initiation of antiplatelet therapy (e.g., aspirin, clopidogrel) or anticoagulation.
Evaluation for potential need for neuroprotective strategies or further interventions.
reason_for_urgency: Acute ischemic stroke with large vessel occlusion (M1 MCA) requiring emergent reperfusion therapy. Requires immediate neurological evaluation, monitoring for complications post-thrombectomy, and urgent decisions regarding secondary prevention.
